pr-dashboard.template: Run the PR dashboard script: `bun run ~/.config/opencode/scripts/pr-dashboard.ts`. Report what PRs were found, what was written to the daily note, and any agent sessions spawned. If the user passes arguments, append them to the command (e.g. `--no-agents` or `--dry-run`).
pr-dashboard.description: Fetch PRs to review and PRs needing changes, update daily note

yesmand-once.template: Run one yesmand issue cycle: `bun run ~/dotfiles/yesmand/src/index.ts --once --config ~/dotfiles/yesmand/config.json`. Report what candidates were found, what was dispatched, and any OpenCode session IDs created. If the user passes arguments, append them to this command (e.g. `--dry-run` or `--debug`).

rebase.template: Rebase the current branch onto the upstream default branch. Steps: 1) Run `git fetch origin`. 2) Determine the default branch by checking if `origin/main` exists (`git rev-parse --verify origin/main`), falling back to `origin/master`. 3) Run `git rebase origin/<default-branch>`. 4) If there are merge conflicts, examine each conflicted file, resolve the conflicts intelligently by keeping the intent of both changes where possible, then `git add` the resolved files and run `git rebase --continue`. Repeat until the rebase completes. If the user passes arguments, use them (e.g. `--abort` to cancel an in-progress rebase). 5) when done force push with lease
rebase.description: Rebase current branch onto origin main/master, resolving conflicts
